layman_headline: The crypto market is fearful and alts are bleeding — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is sitting in 'Fear' territory at 26/100, and Bitcoin dominance is high at 56.3%, which means money is flowing out of smaller coins and into Bitcoin. Most of the swing trade candidates on the watchlist are in downtrends with bearish chart structures. While a few coins like ZRO are approaching oversold levels that could trigger a bounce, none of them have the combination of strong technicals, volume confirmation, and a clear catalyst needed to justify risking capital right now.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines for now. Keep cash ready and watch for ZRO or ETH-related plays to reach true oversold conditions with a volume spike before committing. There's no rush — fearful markets reward patience.
expert_reasoning: Across the entire candidate list, no coin presents a compelling risk-reward swing entry. ZRO's RSI at 30.8 is the most oversold reading, but its chart bias is -0.70 (bearish), it's below all major moving averages, and the risk/reward ratio on the chart shows a very tight support at $0.90 that could easily break. HTX has the best chart bias (+1.10) with a Bullish Marubozu, but its RSI at 58 signals it's already extended, the 7-day gain of 8.7% suggests the move may be largely priced in, and the micro-cap nature with near-zero price makes it extremely risky. ETHFI shows a +6% 24h bounce with a bullish chart (+0.60), but resistance at $0.40 is only 2.5% away — terrible risk/reward. The macro backdrop with BTC dominance at 56.3% and Fear & Greed at 26 strongly argues against alt entries. The CoinTelegraph headline about 'late stages of bear market' is notable but doesn't constitute an actionable catalyst yet.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.3% and rising signals continued capital rotation away from alts — any alt entry faces macro headwinds
U.S. launching fresh Iran strikes adds geopolitical uncertainty that could trigger a broader risk-off selloff
No volume confirmation or MACD crosses on any candidate — all potential setups lack technical confirmation
watch_list: ZRO RSI dropping below 30 with a volume spike — would signal true capitulation and a higher-probability bounce entry near $0.85
BTC behavior around $63,000-$64,000 range — a decisive move higher could relieve alt pressure; a breakdown would accelerate alt selling
ETH derivatives reset (per Blockworks) — could create a clean long setup for ETH-related plays like ETHFI if funding resets to negative
market_context: The crypto market is in a fear state (26/100) with BTC dominance at 56.3%, creating a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades. Geopolitical tensions from U.S.-Iran strikes and stalled crypto IPO momentum add macro uncertainty, though CoinTelegraph's 'late stages of bear market' thesis and improving Fear & Greed trend (from 24 weekly avg to 26) suggest conditions may be nearing a turn.
context_banner: Fear-driven market with high BTC dominance demands patience — no altcoin setup clears the quality bar for capital deployment today.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 26 ('Fear') with improving trend from 24 weekly average — not yet at extreme fear levels that historically mark the best entries.
ZRO RSI at 30.8 is the most oversold candidate, approaching the 30 threshold that historically signals bounce setups, but not yet confirmed.
Jamie Coutts/Real Vision analysis suggests Bitcoin is nearing 'late stages of bear market' — a macro-level signal that conditions may turn but timing remains uncertain.
U.S. fresh Iran strikes and stalled crypto IPO market add geopolitical and institutional headwinds that weigh against risk-on positioning.
ETH derivatives reset story suggests positioning is being cleaned out — a precondition for the next leg up in ETH-ecosystem tokens.
